commands.author: jason-allen-oneal

target.title: fix: add inbound peer send policy matching
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds native `session.sendPolicy.rules[].match.peerEquals: "inboundPeer"` support.
- Allows relational send-policy checks between the outbound target and the inbound peer for the current automatic-delivery turn, including `identityLinks` aliases.
- Threads inbound peer context through automatic replies, routed replies, tool context, plugin/gateway message-action context, and durable outbound delivery.
- Keeps send-policy-only predicates out of shared allow/deny scopes.
- Preserves explicit `message.send` compatibility by keeping explicit gateway/plugin/direct sends outside `session.sendPolicy` suppression.
- Reports blocked automatic durable sends as `denied_by_send_policy` suppression outcomes.
- Adds focused unit coverage and docs/help labels for the new predicate.

Fixes #85175.

## Verification

Latest head explicit-gateway-send follow-up validated locally: `fa5474b29b`.

Clean local worktree used: `~/projects/openclaw-pr-85763-send-policy`.

Commands run against latest head:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile
pnpm exec vitest run src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts
pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts
```

Earlier broad validation on head `5b0f312431` before the schema-only follow-up:

```text
pnpm protocol:check
pnpm lint
pnpm check:test-types
OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts
```

Observed local results on latest head:

```text
pnpm install --frozen-lockfile already up to date
src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts passed 13/13 tests
src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts passed 21/21 tests
test/scripts/real-behavior-proof-policy.test.ts passed 23/23 tests
```

Earlier broad validation results on head `5b0f312431`:

```text
pnpm protocol:check passed
pnpm lint passed with 0 warnings and 0 errors
pnpm check:test-types passed
src/gateway/server-startup-post-attach.test.ts passed 39/39 tests
```

Earlier focused send-policy validation on this branch:

```text
pnpm test src/sessions/send-policy.test.ts src/config/schema.hints.test.ts src/cli/config-cli.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message.test.ts src/infra/outbound/outbound-send-service.test.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.plugin-dispatch.test.ts
pnpm check:changed
```

## Real behavior proof

Latest head under test: `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`
Proof id: `oc-pr85763-20260524164843`
Run time: `2026-05-24T16:48:43Z`
Proof comment: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/85763#issuecomment-4529388676

### Config under test

`session.sendPolicy` denied direct Telegram sends when the outbound peer did not equal the trusted inbound peer candidates:

```json
{
  "default": "allow",
  "rules": [
    {
      "action": "deny",
      "match": {
        "allOf": [
          { "channel": "telegram" },
          { "chatType": "direct" },
          { "peerEquals": "inboundPeer", "invert": true }
        ]
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### Live Telegram setup

- Telegram bot channel was configured and paired.
- A live inbound Telegram direct session was created for redacted `TG_PEER_A`.
- PR-head local source checkout: `e7b028ba5aa2c7444937deb020e838e130945f70`.
- The proof used PR-head `sendDurableMessageBatch` with a Telegram outbound proof adapter. The adapter performed a real Bot API `sendMessage` for allowed sends and recorded whether a wrong-peer send attempted any Telegram API call.
- `skipQueue=true` was used to avoid mutating the delivery queue during proof; the durable send-policy path still executed before platform send.

### Redacted transcript

```text
Inbound Telegram direct session: agent:main:telegram:direct:TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC
Inbound peer candidates: TG_PEER_A, TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC, TG_PEER_A_USERNAME

Same-peer allow:
  outbound target: TG_PEER_A
  durable result: sent
  Telegram message id: 6
  Telegram API calls for this case: 1

Wrong-peer suppression:
  attempted outbound target: TG_PEER_B
  durable result: suppressed
  suppression reason: denied_by_send_policy
  cancel reason: send_policy_peer_mismatch
  Telegram API calls for this case: 0
```

### Redacted diagnostic excerpt

```json
{
  "samePeerAllow": {
    "target": "TG_PEER_A",
    "result": {
      "status": "sent",
      "results": [
        { "channel": "telegram", "messageId": "6", "chatId": "TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC" }
      ]
    },
    "telegramApiCallsForCase": 1
  },
  "wrongPeerSuppression": {
    "target": "TG_PEER_B",
    "result": {
      "status": "suppressed",
      "reason": "denied_by_send_policy",
      "payloadOutcomes": [
        {
          "status": "suppressed",
          "reason": "denied_by_send_policy",
          "hookEffect": {
            "cancelReason": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
            "metadata": {
              "code": "send_policy_peer_mismatch",
              "peerEquals": "inboundPeer",
              "expectedPeers": ["TG_PEER_A", "TG_PEER_A_NUMERIC", "TG_PEER_A_USERNAME"],
              "actualPeer": "TG_PEER_B"
            }
          }
        }
      ]
    },
    "telegramApiCallsForCase": 0
  }
}
```

### Proof verdict

- Same-peer Telegram durable send to `TG_PEER_A` was delivered.
- Wrong-peer durable outbound attempt to `TG_PEER_B` was suppressed by `session.sendPolicy`.
- Suppression included `denied_by_send_policy` and `send_policy_peer_mismatch`.
- The wrong-peer case recorded zero Telegram API send calls, so `TG_PEER_B` received no Telegram message.
- Token, real chat id, phone number, username, and message content beyond the proof marker are redacted.
